top_module.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
top_module.description: Top-level memory-mapped scratchpad controller with a built-in BIST request/status path and explicit instantiation of the SRAM wrapper as child instance u_sram.
top_module.functionality: Implements a small register-mapped SRAM scratchpad controller with explicit memory intent for a 256x32 macro. The controller accepts direct firmware write/read strobes, provides a software-visible memory address and data path, latches BIST start requests from either the external bist_start input or the BIST_CONTROL register, exposes BIST status and IRQ status registers, and drives a child SRAM wrapper instance named u_sram. The controller must remain synthesizable and keep the memory macro intent visible to downstream synthesis and DFT/MBIST agents.
responsibilities: Accept direct write and read accesses through wr_en/wr_addr/wr_data and rd_en/rd_addr.
Expose a software-accessible register map at the specified offsets.
Drive ready high after reset completion and when the controller is not busy with BIST sequencing.
Latch BIST done/fail state and last_fail_addr for status reporting.
Assert irq when IRQ_ENABLE is set and a BIST done/fail condition is latched.
Instantiate the SRAM wrapper as child instance u_sram.
Preserve explicit Sky130 prebuilt SRAM macro intent for downstream flow detection.
Allow downstream MBIST collateral to replace or wrap the memory path without changing the top-level controller interface.
top_module.reset_behavior: On reset, clear CONTROL, STATUS, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and any internal ready/busy/BIST-latched state. Deassert irq, deassert bist_done and bist_fail, and set ready to the defined reset-safe state. The SRAM contents are not required to be cleared by reset.
behavior_rules: Register map offsets are 0x00 CONTROL, 0x04 STATUS, 0x08 MEM_ADDR, 0x0C MEM_WDATA, 0x10 MEM_RDATA, 0x14 MEM_CONTROL, 0x18 BIST_CONTROL, 0x1C BIST_STATUS, 0x20 IRQ_STATUS, 0x24 IRQ_CLEAR.
CONTROL bit 0 is ENABLE, bit 1 is SOFT_RESET, bit 2 is IRQ_ENABLE.
STATUS exposes ready, bist_done, bist_fail, busy.
MEM_CONTROL bit 0 requests a memory write and bit 1 requests a memory read.
BIST_CONTROL bit 0 requests BIST start and bit 1 clears BIST results.
BIST_STATUS exposes done, fail, running, and last_fail_addr[7:0].
IRQ_STATUS latches bist_done and bist_fail conditions until cleared by IRQ_CLEAR.
IRQ_CLEAR is write-one-to-clear for IRQ_STATUS bits.
Direct memory accesses should use the wrapper interface with one functional access path.
If the underlying macro collateral includes a second read port, it remains reserved and tied off or isolated in the wrapper contract.
The wrapper module demo_sram_32x256_wrapper must be instantiated as child instance u_sram by the top controller.
The fallback demo_sram_32x256_model must preserve the same clk/csb/web/addr/din/dout interface for replacement or simulation.
The controller should be fast to run and keep the implementation simple, with explicit memory intent retained for macro-aware tooling.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x256_wrapper
modules.description: Functional SRAM wrapper that presents the canonical simple scratchpad interface and binds either to the prebuilt Sky130 SRAM macro or to the synthesizable fallback model while keeping the memory intent explicit.
modules.functionality: Provides a stable SRAM-like wrapper interface for a 256x32 memory. The wrapper is the contract point used by the top controller and may internally instantiate either the prebuilt Sky130 SRAM macro or the synthesizable fallback model depending on collateral availability. The wrapper keeps the macro name, dimensions, and MBIST expectation explicit to downstream tools.
responsibilities: Expose a canonical scratchpad wrapper interface.
Bind to the prebuilt Sky130 SRAM macro when macro collateral is available.
Bind to the fallback synthesizable model when macro collateral is unavailable.
Preserve explicit macro naming and dimensions for synthesis and DFT/MBIST detection.
Provide a single functional access path for the top controller.
modules.reset_behavior: The wrapper itself has no architectural state beyond its child binding choice. Any internal state follows the selected child memory implementation. Read data is not required to be cleared by reset.
behavior_rules: Preserve the exact wrapper port names clk, csb, web, addr, din, and dout.
When bound to the prebuilt macro, preserve the macro identity sky130_sram_1kbyte_1rw1r_32x256_8.
When bound to the fallback model, preserve identical functional behavior for simulation and synthesis.
If a second read port exists in the underlying macro collateral, keep it reserved for MBIST wrapper integration and do not expose it as a top-level controller requirement.
csb is active-low chip select and web is active-low write enable for the functional wrapper contract.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x256_model
modules.description: Synthesizable 256x32 SRAM fallback model used only when the prebuilt macro collateral is unavailable, with the same wrapper-facing interface for seamless replacement.
modules.functionality: Implements a synthesizable memory array fallback with the same functional interface expected by the wrapper. On write cycles, when csb is low and web is low, store din at addr on the active clock edge. On read cycles, when csb is low and web is high, return the addressed word through dout according to a simple synchronous memory model suitable for simulation and synthesis fallback. Keep the implementation generic and free of vendor-only primitives.
responsibilities: Provide a simple synthesizable memory fallback.
Support one write-access path and one read-access path.
Keep memory contents at 256 words by 32 bits.
modules.reset_behavior: Memory contents may be left uninitialized or initialized to a synthesis-safe default as allowed by the target flow. Read data should be in a known safe state after reset where practical, but the design contract does not require clearing all stored memory words.
behavior_rules: Preserve the exact port interface used by the wrapper.
Keep the model synthesizable.
Do not use vendor-only SRAM primitives.
Do not change the 256x32 capacity or the 8-bit address width.
The model must be functionally replaceable by macro-aware flows.

top_level_connections.description: Top-level port clk connected to matching child module port(s).
